name: Tin Can Mailman
tagline: Kaimuki's treasure trove of vintage Hawaiiana — old maps, postcards, books, and ephemera that tell Hawaii's story.
bestTime: Weekday afternoons for unhurried browsing — the owner loves to talk story
talkStory: Named after the tin cans that 19th-century sailors used to leave mail on remote Pacific beaches, this Kaimuki shop is a treasure trove of vintage Hawaiiana. Owner Lance Owens has spent decades collecting old maps, postcards, menus, photographs, sheet music, and paper ephemera from Hawaii's past. Find a 1920s Royal Hawaiian Hotel menu, an original Pearl Harbor map, or a first-edition Hawaiian history book. Owens loves to talk story about the history behind each piece -- budget extra time for browsing.
